back:
Pott’s puffy tumor – frontal sinusitis +
osteomyelitis

Adamantinomatous craniopharyngioma
Wet keratin, palisading epithelium, stellate reticulum

Pituitary adenoma
Acidophils (40%: PRL, GH, FSH/LH)
Basophils (10%: ACTH, TSH)
Null cells (50%)

Rathke's cleft cyst
Watery mucous fluid with columnar/cuboidal epithelium
Dura extending between anterior clinoid and planum sphenoidale, covering the optic nerve

Foster-Kennedy syndrome
Olfactory groove meningioma causing:
Ipsilateral anosmia
Ipsilateral optic atrophy (direct compression)
Contralateral papilledema (increased ICP)

front: Describe the classic findings in Foster-Kennedy syndrome
back:
Foster-Kennedy syndrome findings
Olfactory groove meningioma
Ipsilateral anosmia
Ipsilateral optic atrophy
Contralateral papilledema
